blocks:
Sūrat al-Rūm (The Byzantines)

Meccan, and it is 60 Ayahs

Sūrat al-Rūm is Meccan

Except for His saying: فَسُبْحانَ اللهِ حِينَ تُمْسُونَ ** (So glorify Allah when you enter the evening) [Q 30:17]. Its number of verses is sixty verses بِسْمِ اللهِ الرَّحْمنِ الرَّحِيمِ** (In the Name of Allah, the All-beneficent, the All-merciful)

(١) الم (1) (Alif, Lām, Mīm.) (٢) غُلِبَتِ الرُّومُ ** (2) (The Byzantines have been vanquished) Persia vanquished them. (٣) فِي أَدْنَى الْأَرْضِ ** (3) (in the lowest land,) It was said meaning: the nearest land of the Arabs to them, or the nearest of their land to the Arabs. وَهُمْ مِنْ بَعْدِ غَلَبِهِمْ سَيَغْلِبُونَ. (but after their vanquishing, they will vanquish.) (٤) فِي بِضْعِ سِنِينَ لِلَّهِ الْأَمْرُ مِنْ قَبْلُ وَمِنْ بَعْدُ ** (4) (in a few years. The command belongs to Allah before and after.) It was said [The command belongs to Allah] from before their being vanquishers, which is the time of their being vanquished. Meaning: the command belongs to Him when they were vanquished and when they vanquish; nothing of the two occurs except by His decree. In al-Khara'ij, narrated from al-Zakiyy (peace be upon him): He (peace be upon him) was asked about it, and He said: "The command (الْأَمْرُ) belongs to Him from before He commands it, and the command belongs to Him مِنْ بَعْدُ ** (after) He commands it. He decrees whatever He wills." And al-Qummi, narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): لِلَّهِ الْأَمْرُ مِنْ قَبْلُ ** (The command belongs to Allah before) He commands, وَمِنْ بَعْدُ ** (and after) He decrees whatever He wills. وَيَوْمَئِذٍ ** (And on that day)—meaning the day they vanquish—يَفْرَحُ الْمُؤْمِنُونَ.** (the faithful will rejoice.)

بِنَصْرِ اللهِ يَنْصُرُ مَنْ يَشاءُ ** (5) by Allah’s help. He helps whomever He wishes. So He helps these (people) sometimes and those (people) at other times. وَهُوَ الْعَزِيزُ الرَّحِيمُ ** and He is the All-mighty, the All-merciful. He takes vengeance upon His servants by granting victory over them sometimes, and He bestows favor upon them by granting them victory at other times.

It was said: Persia defeated the Romans and prevailed over them during the time of the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم. The disbelievers of Quraysh rejoiced at this because the people of Persia, like Quraysh, were not People of the Book (أهل كتاب). This grieved the Muslims. Bayt al-Maqdis (بيت المقدس) was a house for the people of Rome, just as the Ka'bah (الكعبة) was for the Muslims, and Persia drove them out of it. Then the Romans prevailed over Persia on the Day of al-Hudaybiyyah (يوم الحديبية).

In al-Kafi, narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): He was asked about this verse, and he said: "Indeed, it has an interpretation (تأويلاً) which none knows save Allah and those firmly rooted in knowledge (الرّاسخون في العلم) from the Family of Muhammad (may the blessings of Allah be upon them). When the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم migrated to Medina and manifested Islam, He wrote a letter to the King of Rome and sent it with a messenger, inviting him to Islam. He also wrote a letter to the King of Persia, inviting him to Islam, and sent it to him with His messenger. As for the King of Rome, he honored the letter of the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم and respected His messenger. But as for the King of Persia, he treated the letter of the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم lightly, tore it up, and treated His messenger lightly. At that time, the King of Persia was fighting the King of Rome. The Muslims desired that the King of Rome should defeat the King of Persia, and they were more inclined towards the Romans than the King of Persia. So, when the King of Persia defeated the King of Rome, the Muslims disliked that and were grieved by it. Thus, Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, sent down a Book concerning that: الم (١) غُلِبَتِ الرُّومُ (٢) فِي أَدْنَى الْأَرْضِ (٣) (1) Alif. Lām. Mīm. (2) The Romans have been vanquished (3) in the nearest land. Meaning: Persia defeated them فِي أَدْنَى الْأَرْضِ (in the nearest land), which is al-Shamat (الشّامات) and its surroundings. وَهُمْ (and they) — meaning Persia — مِنْ بَعْدِ غَلَبِهِمْ ** (after their vanquishing) — meaning the Romans — سَيَغْلِبُونَ ** (will be vanquished) — meaning the Muslims will vanquish them — فِي بِضْعِ سِنِينَ لِلَّهِ الْأَمْرُ مِنْ قَبْلُ وَمِنْ بَعْدُ وَيَوْمَئِذٍ يَفْرَحُ الْمُؤْمِنُونَ بِنَصْرِ اللهِ يَنْصُرُ مَنْ يَشاءُ. (4) in a few years. To Allah belongs the command, before and after, and on that day the faithful will rejoice (5) at Allah’s help. He helps whomever He wishes.

He (peace be upon him) said: So, when the Muslims invaded Persia and conquered it, the Muslims rejoiced at the help of Allah, the Mighty and Majestic. It was asked: "Does not Allah say فِي بِضْعِ سِنِينَ ** (in a few years), yet many years passed for the believers with the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم and during the caliphate (إمارة) of Abu Bakr, and the believers only defeated Persia during the caliphate (إمارة) of Umar?" He replied: "Did I not tell you that this has an interpretation (تأويلاً) and an exegesis (تفسيراً), and that the Quran has abrogating (ناسخ) and abrogated (منسوخ) verses? Do you not hear the saying of Allah, the Mighty and Majestic: لِلَّهِ الْأَمْرُ مِنْ قَبْلُ وَمِنْ بَعْدُ ** (To Allah belongs the command, before and after)? Meaning: To Him belongs the Will (المشيّة) regarding the saying: that He may delay what He has put forward, and put forward what He has delayed in the saying, until the day when the decree (القضاء) was finalized for the descent of victory upon the believers therein. That is His saying, the Mighty and Majestic: **وَيَوْمَئِذٍ يَفْرَحُ الْمُؤْمِنُونَ بِنَصْرِ اللهِ ** (and on that day the faithful will rejoice at Allah’s help), meaning: the day when the decree of victory was finalized."

And in al-Qummi, narrated from him (peace be upon him): A similar narration, except that he did not mention His saying: يعني يغلبهم المسلمون (meaning the Muslims will vanquish them), nor His saying: فلمّا غزا المسلمون (So, when the Muslims invaded) up to His saying: بِنَصْرِ اللهِ ** (at Allah’s help). The structure (بناء) of the two narrations is based on the reading سيُغلبون (سيُغلبون) with a dammah (ضمّ) on the ya (الياء), along with the dammah on غُلِبَتِ (غُلِبَتِ). It was read (وقرئ) in the irregular readings (الشّواذّ) غَلَبَتِ (غَلَبَتِ) with a fathah (الفتح) and سيَغْلِبُونَ (سيَغْلِبُونَ) with a dammah (الضمّ). The structure of what is in the book al-Istighathah (الاستغاثة) by Ibn Maytham is based on this (latter reading). He said: We have narrated through the path of the scholars (علماء) of the People of the House (أهل البيت) regarding their secrets (أسرارهم) and their knowledge (علومهم) which emerged from them to the scholars (علماء) of their followers (شيعتهم): that there are a people who are attributed to Quraysh but are not truly from Quraysh by lineage (النّسب), and this is something known only by the source of Prophethood (معدن النبوّة) and the inheritors of the knowledge of the Message (ورثة علم الرسالة). This is like Banu Umayyah (بني أميّة). They mentioned that they are not from Quraysh and that their origin is from the Romans (الروم). The interpretation (تأويل) of this verse, الم غُلِبَتِ الرُّومُ (1. Alif. Lām. Mīm. 2. The Romans have been vanquished), applies to them. Its meaning is that they were vanquished regarding the kingship (الملك), and Banu al-Abbas (بنو العبّاس) will vanquish them regarding that.

(٦) وَعْدَ اللهِ لا يُخْلِفُ اللهُ وَعْدَهُ وَلكِنَّ أَكْثَرَ النَّاسِ لا يَعْلَمُونَ. (6) (It is) Allah’s promise: Allah does not break His promise, but most people do not know. (٧) يَعْلَمُونَ ظاهِراً مِنَ الْحَياةِ الدُّنْيا ما يشاهدون منها وَهُمْ عَنِ الْآخِرَةِ التي هي غايتها والمقصود منها وهُمْ غافِلُونَ لا تخطر ببالهم. (7) They know the surface of the life of the world, what they observe of it, and they are about the Hereafter which is its ultimate goal (غايتها) and the intended purpose (المقصود منها) of it, and they are heedless (غافِلُونَ), it does not cross their minds. Al-Qummi said: They see the present of the world and they are heedless (يتغافلون) of the Hereafter. And in al-Majma', narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): He was asked concerning the Almighty's saying: يَعْلَمُونَ ظاهِراً مِنَ الْحَياةِ الدُّنْيا ** (They know the surface of the life of the world), so He said: Part of it is divination (الزّجر) and astrology (النّجوم).

(8) أَوَلَمْ يَتَفَكَّرُوا فِي أَنْفُسِهِمْ ** (8) Have they not reflected in their own souls? Have they not initiated reflection (التفكُّر) concerning them (their souls)? Or, have they not reflected upon the matter (امر) of their souls? For indeed, they are closer to them than anything else, and they are a mirror (مِرآة) wherein is manifested to the insightful (المُستبصر) what is manifested to him in the rest (ساير) of the created things (المخلوقات), so that the power of their Originator (مبدعها) over their restoration (إعادتها) may be realized by them, just as His power is over their origination (إبداعها). ما خَلَقَ اللهُ السَّماواتِ وَالْأَرْضَ وَما بَيْنَهُما إِلَّا بِالْحَقِّ وَأَجَلٍ مُسَمًّى ** (8) Allah did not create the heavens and the earth and what is between them except with the truth and for a specified term. which ends at it (the term) and does not remain after it. وَإِنَّ كَثِيراً مِنَ النَّاسِ بِلِقاءِ رَبِّهِمْ لَكافِرُونَ ** (8) Indeed, many people are faithless to the encounter with their Lord. Deniers (جاحدون), who assume that the world (الدنيا) is eternal (أبديّة) and that the Hereafter (الآخرة) will not exist.

(٩) أَوَلَمْ يَسِيرُوا فِي الْأَرْضِ فَيَنْظُرُوا كَيْفَ كانَ عاقِبَةُ الَّذِينَ مِنْ قَبْلِهِمْ ** (Have they not travelled in the land so that they may observe how was the fate of those who were before them?) This is an affirmation of their traveling through the regions (أقطار) of the earth and their observation of the remnants (آثار) of those destroyed before them.

And in al-Khisal, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): Its meaning is: "Have they not looked into the Quran?" كانُوا أَشَدَّ مِنْهُمْ قُوَّةً (They were mightier than them in strength) like 'Ad and Thamud, وَأَثارُوا الْأَرْضَ (and they tilled the earth) and turned over its surface (وجهها) for the extraction of water (لاستنباط المياه), the mining of minerals (واستخراج المعادن), the planting of seeds (وزرع البذور), and other things, وَعَمَرُوها (and they cultivated it) and they cultivated the earth أَكْثَرَ مِمَّا عَمَرُوها ** (more than they cultivated it) from the cultivation of the people of Mecca of it, for they are the people of a valley without cultivation (واد غير ذي زرع); they are not given abundance (لا تبسط لهم) in other than it. And in this, there is mockery (تهكّم) of them, in that they are deluded (مغترّون) by the world and boastful of it, while they are weaker in condition (أضعف حالاً) within it. وَجاءَتْهُمْ رُسُلُهُمْ بِالْبَيِّناتِ ** (and their apostles came to them with manifest proofs) with the clear signs (الآيات الواضحات). فَما كانَ اللهُ لِيَظْلِمَهُمْ ** (So Allah would not wrong them) by destroying them without crime (جرم) or reminder (تذكير). وَلَكِنْ كانُوا أَنْفُسَهُمْ يَظْلِمُونَ** (but they used to wrong themselves) since they committed what led to their destruction.

(١٠) ثُمَّ كانَ عاقِبَةَ الَّذِينَ أَساؤُا السُّواى ** (Then the consequence of those who committed evil was the evilest consequence) It was said: meaning, then their consequence was the punishment (العقوبة). The explicit noun (الظاهر) was placed in the position of the pronoun (الضمير) to indicate what necessitated that that should be their consequence. And السُّواى ** is the feminine form of aswa' (أسوء). And it was read 'āqibata (عاقبة) with the nasb (النصب). أَنْ كَذَّبُوا بِآياتِ اللهِ وَكانُوا بِها يَسْتَهْزِؤُنَ (that they denied the signs of Allah and used to ridicule them.) It was said: an kadhdhabū (أَنْ كَذَّبُوا) is either a substitute (بدل) or it is the predicate (خبر) of kāna (كان). And al-Sū' (السّوأ) is the verbal noun (مصدر) of asā'ū (أساءوا) or its object (مفعول), meaning: ثُمَّ كانَ عاقِبَةَ الَّذِينَ ** (Then the consequence of those who) committed the sin (الخطيئة) was that Allah sealed their hearts until they denied the signs and ridiculed them.

(١١) اللهُ يَبْدَؤُا الْخَلْقَ ثُمَّ يُعِيدُهُ ثُمَّ إِلَيْهِ تُرْجَعُونَ (11) (11) Allah originates the creation, then He reproduces it, then to Him you will be brought back. He originates them (ينشئهم) ثُمَّ يُعِيدُهُ ** He resurrects them (يبعثهم) ثُمَّ إِلَيْهِ تُرْجَعُونَ ** for the recompense (للجزاء). And it was recited (وقرئ) with the letter yā’ (بالياء).

(١٢) وَيَوْمَ تَقُومُ السَّاعَةُ يُبْلِسُ الْمُجْرِمُونَ (12) (12) And on the day when the Hour commences, the guilty will be dumbfounded. They will be silent (يسكتون), bewildered (متحيّرين), and despairing (آيسين).

(١٣) وَلَمْ يَكُنْ لَهُمْ مِنْ شُرَكائِهِمْ شُفَعاءُ وَكانُوا بِشُرَكائِهِمْ كافِرِينَ (13) (13) And they will have no intercessors from among their partners, and they will disbelieve in their partners. وَلَمْ يَكُنْ لَهُمْ مِنْ شُرَكائِهِمْ ** from those whom they associated with Allah (ممّن اشركوهم بالله) شُفَعاءُ ** who might grant them protection (يجيرونهم) from the punishment of Allah وَكانُوا بِشُرَكائِهِمْ كافِرِينَ.

(١٤) وَيَوْمَ تَقُومُ السَّاعَةُ يَوْمَئِذٍ يَتَفَرَّقُونَ (14) (14) And on the day when the Hour commences, on that day they will be separated. Al-Qummi (القمّيّ) said: To Paradise and the Fire (إلى الجنّة والنار).

(١٥) فَأَمَّا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا وَعَمِلُوا الصَّالِحاتِ فَهُمْ فِي رَوْضَةٍ يُحْبَرُونَ (15) (15) As for those who have faith and do righteous deeds, they will be rejoicing in a garden. Al-Qummi (القمّيّ) [said]: meaning, they will be honored (يكرمون), and its origin (أصله) is joy (السّرور).

(١٦) وَأَمَّا الَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا وَكَذَّبُوا بِآياتِنا وَلِقاءِ الْآخِرَةِ فَأُولئِكَ فِي الْعَذابِ مُحْضَرُونَ (16) (16) But as for those who were faithless and denied Our signs and the encounter of the Hereafter, they will be arraigned in the punishment. They will not be absent from it (لا يغيبون عنه).

(١٧) فَسُبْحانَ اللهِ حِينَ تُمْسُونَ وَحِينَ تُصْبِحُونَ (17) (17) So glorify Allah when you enter the evening and when you enter the morning.

(١٨) وَلَهُ الْحَمْدُ فِي السَّماواتِ وَالْأَرْضِ وَعَشِيًّا وَحِينَ تُظْهِرُونَ (18) (18) All praise belongs to Him in the heavens and the earth, in the evening, and when you enter the noon. It was said (قيل): [This is] information (إخبار) in the meaning of a command (الأمر) to glorify Allah, the Glorious and Exalted (الله سبحانه وتعالى), and to praise Him during these times when His power (قدرته) becomes manifest and His favor (نعمته) is renewed. And it was said (وقيل): The verse encompasses the five prayers (الصلوات الخمس): تُمْسُونَ (when you enter the evening) is the prayer of al-Maghrib (صلاة المغرب) and al-Isha (والعشاء); تُصْبِحُونَ (when you enter the morning) is the prayer of al-Fajr (صلاة الفجر); عَشِيًّا (in the evening) is the prayer of al-Asr (صلاة العصر); and تُظْهِرُونَ (when you enter the noon) is the prayer of al-Dhuhr (صلاة الظهر).

(١٩) يُخْرِجُ الْحَيَّ مِنَ الْمَيِّتِ وَيُخْرِجُ الْمَيِّتَ مِنَ الْحَيِ. (19) He brings forth the living from the dead, and brings forth the dead from the living. Al-Qummi said: يُخْرِجُ ** (He brings forth) the believer (المؤمن) from the disbeliever (الكافر) and brings forth the disbeliever (الكافر) from the believer (المؤمن). And he narrated it in al-Majma' from both of them (peace be upon them) as previously mentioned. وَيُحْيِ الْأَرْضَ بَعْدَ مَوْتِها وَكَذلِكَ تُخْرَجُونَ ** (and revives the earth after its death. Likewise, you will be brought forth) from your graves (قبوركم). And it was recited with the fathah on the tā' (ت). In al-Kafi, narrated from al-Kazim (peace be upon him) regarding His saying: يُحْيِ الْأَرْضَ بَعْدَ مَوْتِها ** (and revives the earth after its death). He said: "He does not revive it by rain (القطر), but rather Allah sends forth men (رجالاً) who revive justice (العدل), and thus the earth is revived due to the revival of justice. And the establishment of the prescribed punishment (الحدّ) therein is more beneficial for the earth than rain (القطر) for forty mornings (صباحاً)."

(٢٠) وَمِنْ آياتِهِ أَنْ خَلَقَكُمْ مِنْ تُرابٍ ثُمَّ إِذا أَنْتُمْ بَشَرٌ تَنْتَشِرُونَ. (20) And among His signs is that He created you from dust, then behold, you are humans scattering [all over]. (٢١) وَمِنْ آياتِهِ أَنْ خَلَقَ لَكُمْ مِنْ أَنْفُسِكُمْ أَزْواجاً لِتَسْكُنُوا إِلَيْها ** (21) And among His signs is that He created for you mates from your own selves that you may find tranquility in them, That you may incline towards them and find affection through them, for kinship (الجنسيّة) is the cause (علّة) of joining (الضمّ), and difference (الاختلاف) is the reason (سبب) for aversion (التنافر). وَجَعَلَ بَيْنَكُمْ مَوَدَّةً وَرَحْمَةً ** and He put love and mercy between you. by means of marriage (الزّواج). إِنَّ فِي ذلِكَ لَآياتٍ لِقَوْمٍ يَتَفَكَّرُونَ There are indeed signs in that for a people who reflect. so they know the wisdom (الحكم) contained therein.

(٢٢) وَمِنْ آياتِهِ خَلْقُ السَّماواتِ وَالْأَرْضِ وَاخْتِلافُ أَلْسِنَتِكُمْ (22) And among His signs is the creation of the heavens and the earth, and the difference of your languages your languages (لغاتكم) وَأَلْوانِكُمْ and your colors. the whiteness of the skin and its blackness and what is between the two إِنَّ فِي ذلِكَ لَآياتٍ لِلْعالِمِينَ There are indeed signs in that for those who have knowledge. And it was recited with the kasrah on the lām.

In al-Kafi, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him), he said: "The Imam (peace be upon him), when He sees a man, knows him and knows his color. And if He hears his speech from behind a wall, He knows him and knows what he is. Verily, Allah says: وَمِنْ آياتِهِ خَلْقُ السَّماواتِ وَالْأَرْضِ (And among His signs is the creation of the heavens and the earth) [up to] the end of the Ayah." He said: "And they are the knowledgeable ones (العلماء). So He does not hear anything of the matter spoken, except that He knows whether he is saved (ناج) or doomed (هالك). And for that reason, He answers them with that which He answers them."

**(٢٣) وَمِنْ آياتِهِ مَنامُكُمْ بِاللَّيْلِ وَالنَّهارِ وَابْتِغاؤُكُمْ مِنْ فَضْلِهِ مَنامُكُمْ ** (23) And among His signs is your sleep by night and day, and your seeking of His grace. There are indeed signs in that for a people who listen.

[The meaning of] مَنامُكُمْ ** (your sleep) is in the two times (الزّمانين) for the rest of the body and your seeking of your livelihood in them both, or مَنامُكُمْ بِاللَّيْلِ وَابْتِغاؤُكُمْ ** (your sleep by night and your seeking) is by day. So He wrapped and joined the two times (الزّمانين) and the two actions (الفعلين) using two conjunctions (بعاطفين), indicating that each of the two times, even if it is specific to one of the two actions, is suitable for the other when needed. And this is supported by the rest of the verses mentioned concerning it. إِنَّ فِي ذلِكَ لَآياتٍ لِقَوْمٍ يَسْمَعُونَ ** (There are indeed signs in that for a people who listen) means a hearing of understanding (تفهّم) and insight (استبصار), for the wisdom in it is manifest.

(٢٤) وَمِنْ آياتِهِ يُرِيكُمُ الْبَرْقَ خَوْفاً ** (24) And among His signs is that He shows you the lightning, a source of fear of the thunderbolt (الصاعقة) and for the traveler, وَطَمَعاً ** and hope, for the rain (الغيث) and for the resident (المقيم), وَيُنَزِّلُ مِنَ السَّماءِ ماءً فَيُحْيِي بِهِ الْأَرْضَ ** and He sends down water from the sky, and revives the earth with it with vegetation, after its death (مَوْتِها), (meaning) its dryness (يبسها). إِنَّ فِي ذلِكَ لَآياتٍ لِقَوْمٍ يَعْقِلُونَ ** Indeed, there are signs in that for a people who apply reason. (Those who) use their intellects (عقولهم) in deducing its causes (أسبابها) and the manner of its formation (كيفيّة تكوّنها), so that the perfection of the Creator's (الصانع) power and His wisdom (حكمته) becomes manifest to them.

(٢٥) وَمِنْ آياتِهِ أَنْ تَقُومَ السَّماءُ وَالْأَرْضُ بِأَمْرِهِ ** (25) And among His signs is that the heaven and the earth stand by His command. Their standing is by His establishing them and His will for their standing. ثُمَّ إِذا دَعاكُمْ دَعْوَةً مِنَ الْأَرْضِ إِذا أَنْتُمْ تَخْرُجُونَ** Then, when He calls you with a single call from the earth, behold, you will emerge. Then your emergence from the graves suddenly, when He calls you from the earth with a single call, without delay.

(٢٦) وَلَهُ مَنْ فِي السَّماواتِ وَالْأَرْضِ كُلٌّ لَهُ قانِتُونَ ** (26) To Him belongs whoever is in the heavens and the earth. All are obedient to Him. They are submissive to His action concerning them; they do not refuse Him. (٢٧) وَهُوَ الَّذِي يَبْدَؤُا الْخَلْقَ ثُمَّ يُعِيدُهُ ** (27) It is He who originates the creation, then He reproduces it, and that is easier for Him. after their destruction. وَهُوَ أَهْوَنُ عَلَيْهِ ** And the reproduction is easier for Him than the origination, in relation to your capabilities and by analogy to your principles (أصول); otherwise, both are equal to Him. وَلَهُ الْمَثَلُ الْأَعْلى ** And His is the loftiest attribute in the heavens and the earth. The attribute of wondrous significance, which nothing besides Him equals or approaches. In al-Tawhid, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): And to Allah belongs الْمَثَلُ الْأَعْلى ** (the loftiest attribute), which nothing resembles, nor can it be described, nor can it be imagined (يُتَوَهَّم). That is الْمَثَلُ الْأَعْلى (the loftiest attribute). And in al-Uyun, narrated from Imam al-Rida (peace be upon him): Indeed, the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم said to Ali (peace be upon him): "And you are الْمَثَلُ الْأَعْلى (the loftiest attribute)." And in a narration, He (the Prophet) said at the end of His sermon: "We are the word of piety (كَلِمَةُ التَّقْوَى), the path of guidance (سَبِيلُ الْهُدَى), and الْمَثَلُ الْأَعْلى (the loftiest attribute)." And in al-Ziyarah al-Jami'ah al-Jawadiyyah (peace be upon him), the salutation is: "Peace be upon the Imams of Guidance (أَئِمَّةِ الْهُدَى)" up to His saying: "and the inheritors of the Prophets (وَرِثَةُ الْأَنْبِيَاءِ) and الْمَثَلُ الْأَعْلى (the loftiest attribute)." فِي السَّماواتِ وَالْأَرْضِ ** What is in them describes Him by means of indication (دلالة) and utterance (نطقاً). AND HE IS THE ALMIGHTY (الْعَزِيزُ), the Powerful (الْقَادِرُ), Whom origination and reproduction do not render incapable, **الْحَكِيمُ ** (the Wise One), Who executes actions according to the requirement of His wisdom.

(28) ضَرَبَ لَكُمْ مَثَلاً مِنْ أَنْفُسِكُمْ (He draws for you a parable from your own selves,) drawn from your own circumstances, which are the closest matters to you. هَلْ لَكُمْ مِنْ ما مَلَكَتْ أَيْمانُكُمْ (Do you have among those whom your right hands possess) from your slaves مِنْ شُرَكَاءَ فِي ما رَزَقْنَاكُمْ (any partners in what We have provided you) from wealth and other things, فَأَنْتُمْ فِيهِ سَوَاءٌ (so that you are equal in it)? So that you and they are equal in it, disposing of it as you dispose of it, despite the fact that they are human beings like you and that [the wealth] is merely loaned to you. تَخَافُونَهُمْ (Do you fear them) lest you act independently in disposing of it كَخِيفَتِكُمْ أَنْفُسَكُمْ (as you fear one another)? Just as free men fear one another. كَذلِكَ نُفَصِّلُ الْآيَاتِ (Thus do We elaborate the signs)—We make them clear, for indeed, parables are among the things that reveal and clarify meanings—لِقَوْمٍ يَعْقِلُونَ (for a people who apply reason), who use their intellects in reflecting upon the parables.

And al-Qummi (narrated): The reason for its revelation was that when Quraysh and the Arabs performed the pilgrimage (حجّ), they would recite the talbiyah (call). Their talbiyah was: "Here I am, O Allah, here I am. Here I am, no partner have You, here I am. Indeed, all praise, grace, and sovereignty belong to You, no partner have You." This was the talbiyah of Abraham (إبراهيم) and the Prophets (الأنبياء). Then Iblis came to them in the form of an old man (شيخ) and said to them: "This is not the talbiyah of your ancestors (أسلافكم)." They asked: "What was their talbiyah?" He said: "They used to say: 'Here I am, O Allah, here I am. No partner have You, except a partner who belongs to You.'" Quraysh dispersed upon hearing this statement. Iblis then said to them: "Wait (على رِسْلكم)" until he reached the end of his speech. They asked: "What is it?" He said: "Except a partner who belongs to You, You own him and he owns nothing. Do you not see that He owns the partner and what he owns?" So they accepted that, and Quraysh specifically used to recite the talbiyah with this addition. When Allah, the Mighty and Majestic (عزّ وجلّ), sent His Messenger (صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم), He denounced that upon them and said: "This is polytheism (شرك)." So Allah, the Mighty and Majestic (عزّ وجلّ), revealed: ضَرَبَ لَكُمْ مَثَلاً مِنْ أَنْفُسِكُمْ هَلْ لَكُمْ مِنْ ما مَلَكَتْ أَيْمانُكُمْ مِنْ شُرَكَاءَ فِي ما رَزَقْنَاكُمْ فَأَنْتُمْ فِيهِ سَوَاءٌ (He draws for you a parable from your own selves. Do you have among those whom your right hands possess any partners in what We have provided you, so that you are equal in it?) meaning: Do you consent, regarding what you own, that you should have a partner in it? And if you do not consent that you should have a partner in what you own, then how can you consent to set up a partner for Me in what I own?

(٢٩) بَلِ اتَّبَعَ الَّذِينَ ظَلَمُوا ** (29) Nay, the wrongdoers follow by associating partners (بالإشراك) أَهْواءَهُمْ بِغَيْرِ عِلْمٍ ** their own desires without any knowledge. ignorant (جاهلين), nothing restrains them, for the knowledgeable person, if he follows his desire, his knowledge restrains him. فَمَنْ يَهْدِي مَنْ أَضَلَّ اللهُ ** So who will guide him whom Allah has led astray? So who is able to guide Him? وَما لَهُمْ مِنْ ناصِرِينَ ** And they will have no helpers. who rescue them from misguidance (الضلالة) and protect them from its calamities (آفاتها).

(٣٠) فَأَقِمْ وَجْهَكَ لِلدِّينِ حَنِيفاً (1) So set your heart on the religion as a ḥanīf. Al-Qummi (said): Meaning: pure (طاهراً). It was said: This is a metaphor for turning towards it (الإقبال), being steadfast upon it (الاستقامة), and being concerned with it (الاهتمام). In al-Kafi and al-Qummi, narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him), he said: It is the Wilayah (الولاية). In al-Tahdhib, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him), he said: He commanded him to set his face towards the Qiblah (القبلة), in which there is nothing of the worship of idols (الأوثان). And al-Qummi, narrated from him (peace be upon him): He was asked about it, and he said: He stands for the prayer (الصلاة) and does not turn to the right or the left. فِطْرَتَ اللَّهِ الَّتِي فَطَرَ النَّاسَ عَلَيْهَا (1) the original nature of Allah according to which He originated mankind. In al-Kafi, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): He (peace be upon him) was asked about that Fitrah (الفطرة). He said: It is Islam (الإسلام). Allah originated them when He took their covenant (ميثاقهم) upon Monotheism (التوحيد), when He said: "Am I not your Lord?" And among them were the believer (المؤمن) and the disbeliever (الكافر). And narrated from him (peace be upon him): Indeed, Allah created all people upon the Fitrah (الفطرة) upon which He originated them. They did not know faith (ايماناً) through a law (شريعة) nor disbelief (كفراً) through their denial (جحودهم). Then Allah sent the Messengers (الرّسل) inviting the servants to faith in Him. So, among them were those whom Allah guided, and among them were those whom He did not guide. And in it (al-Kafi) and in al-Tawhid, narrated from him (peace be upon him) in many narrations, he said: He originated them upon Monotheism (التوحيد). And narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): He originated them upon the knowledge (المعرفة) of Him. And al-Qummi, narrated from him (peace be upon him), he said: It is لا اله إلّا الله محمّد رسُول الله عليّ وليّ الله (There is no god but Allah, Muhammad is the Messenger of Allah, Ali is the Guardian of Allah). Monotheism (التوحيد) extends up to this point. In al-Basair and al-Tawhid, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him), he said: Upon Monotheism (التوحيد), and Muhammad is the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله, and Ali is the Commander of the Faithful عليه السلام. In al-Tawhid, narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him), he said: He originated them upon Monotheism (التوحيد) at the Covenant (الميثاق), upon the knowledge (معرفته) that He is their Lord. He said: Were it not for that, they would not know who their Lord is, nor who their Sustainer (رازقهم) is. لَا تَبْدِيلَ لِخَلْقِ اللَّهِ (1) There is no altering the creation of Allah. No one is able to change it. ذَلِكَ الدِّينُ الْقَيِّمُ (1) That is the upright religion, the straight one (المستوي) in which there is no crookedness (عوج). وَلَكِنَّ أَكْثَرَ النَّاسِ لَا يَعْلَمُونَ (1) but most people do not know. its uprightness (استقامته).

(٣١) مُنِيبِينَ إِلَيْهِ ** (31) turning to Him penitently. Returning to Him time after time وَاتَّقُوهُ وَأَقِيمُوا الصَّلاةَ وَلا تَكُونُوا مِنَ الْمُشْرِكِينَ and be wary of Him, and maintain the prayer, and do not be among the polytheists, (٣٢) مِنَ الَّذِينَ فَرَّقُوا دِينَهُمْ ** (32) of those who split up their religion They differed concerning what they worship, according to the difference in their desires (أهوائهم). And it was read fāraqū (فارقوا), meaning: they abandoned وَكانُوا شِيَعاً ** and became sects. Sects, each one following its leader (إمامها) who misguided its religion. كُلُّ حِزْبٍ بِما لَدَيْهِمْ فَرِحُونَ** Every faction is happy with what it has. Joyful, thinking that it is the truth.

(٣٣) وَإِذا مَسَّ النَّاسَ ضُرٌّ ** (33) When distress befalls the people, (a hardship) دَعَوْا رَبَّهُمْ مُنِيبِينَ إِلَيْهِ ** they supplicate their Lord, turning to Him penitently. (returning to Him from supplicating others) ثُمَّ إِذا أَذاقَهُمْ مِنْهُ رَحْمَةً Then, when He makes them taste of His mercy, (a deliverance from that hardship) إِذا فَرِيقٌ مِنْهُمْ بِرَبِّهِمْ يُشْرِكُونَ ** behold, a group of them ascribe partners to their Lord, (they suddenly commit الاشراك (association)) بِرَبِّهِمْ ** (to their Lord) (Who granted them well-being (عافاهم)).

(٣٤) لِيَكْفُرُوا بِما آتَيْناهُمْ ** (that they may be ungrateful for what We have given them) The lām (اللّام) in it is for the consequence (للعاقبة). فَتَمَتَّعُوا ** (So enjoy yourselves) A shift in address (التفات). فَسَوْفَ تَعْلَمُونَ ** (soon you will know) [Meaning:] the consequence of your enjoyment. (٣٥) أَمْ أَنْزَلْنا عَلَيْهِمْ سُلْطاناً ** (Did We send down to them any authority) [Meaning:] a proof (حجّة) or one possessing authority (ذا سلطان), meaning: one who has a clear proof (برهان). فَهُوَ يَتَكَلَّمُ بِما كانُوا بِهِ يُشْرِكُونَ (which speaks of what they associate with Him?) [Meaning:] with their association [of partners with Allah] (باشراكهم). (٣٦) وَإِذا أَذَقْنَا النَّاسَ رَحْمَةً ** (When We let people taste a mercy) [Meaning:] a blessing (نعمة) such as health (صحّة) or abundance (سعة). فَرِحُوا بِها ** (they rejoice in it) [Meaning:] they became insolent (بطروا) because of it. وَإِنْ تُصِبْهُمْ سَيِّئَةٌ ** (but should an ill befall them) [Meaning:] hardship (شدّة). بِما قَدَّمَتْ أَيْدِيهِمْ ** (because of what their hands have sent ahead) [Meaning:] due to the ill-omen (بشؤم) of their acts of disobedience (معاصيهم). إِذا هُمْ يَقْنَطُونَ ** (behold, they despair.) [Meaning:] from His mercy. وقرئ with the kasrah (بكسر) on the nūn (النون).

(٣٧) أَوَلَمْ يَرَوْا أَنَّ اللهَ يَبْسُطُ الرِّزْقَ لِمَنْ يَشاءُ وَيَقْدِرُ ** (37) Do they not see that Allah expands the provision for whomever He wishes, and tightens it? So what is wrong with them that they do not show gratitude and do not seek reward (from Allah) in prosperity and adversity, like the believers? إِنَّ فِي ذلِكَ لَآياتٍ لِقَوْمٍ يُؤْمِنُونَ ** Indeed, there are signs in that for a people who have faith. They deduce through them (the signs) the perfection of power (القدرة) and wisdom (الحكمة).

فَآتِ ذَا الْقُرْبى حَقَّهُ وَالْمِسْكِينَ وَابْنَ السَّبِيلِ ذلِكَ خَيْرٌ لِلَّذِينَ يُرِيدُونَ وَجْهَ اللهِ ** (So give the relative his due, and the needy and the traveller. That is better for those who seek Allah’s Face) They intend by their good deeds Him (Allah) purely وَأُولئِكَ هُمُ الْمُفْلِحُونَ ** (and it is they who are the felicitous) since they attained the everlasting bliss (النّعيم المقيم) through what was spread out for them.

In المجمع, narrated from the two of them (peace be upon them): That when this verse was revealed upon the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم, He gave Fatimah فدكاً and delivered it to her. The discussion regarding this meaning has already preceded fully in Surat Bani Isra'il.

(٣٩) وَما آتَيْتُمْ مِنْ رِباً ** (And whatever you pay as usury) (a gift by which an increase in recompense is expected). And it was read (وقرئ) as أتيتم (atiytum) with al-qasr (shortening). لِيَرْبُوَا فِي أَمْوالِ النَّاسِ ** (that it may increase in people’s wealth,) so that it may increase and purify in their wealth, meaning it grows therein and then returns to him. And it was read (وقرئ) with the tā’ (ت) having a ḍamma and the wāw (و) being sākin (silent): فَلا يَرْبُوا عِنْدَ اللهِ (it will not increase with Allah.) so it will not purify with Him, meaning no reward will be given for it from Allah.

In al-Kafi, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him), he said: "Usury (الرّبا) is of two types: usury that is consumed (يؤكل) and usury that is not consumed (لا يؤكل). As for that which is consumed, it is your gift to a man from whom you seek a recompense better than it. That is the usury that is consumed, and it is the saying of Allah, the Mighty and Majestic: **وَما آتَيْتُمْ مِنْ رِباً لِيَرْبُوَا فِي أَمْوالِ النَّاسِ فَلا يَرْبُوا عِنْدَ اللهِ ** (And whatever you pay as usury that it may increase in people’s wealth, it will not increase with Allah.) And as for that which is not consumed, it is that which Allah has forbidden and for which He has promised the Fire."

And in al-Qummi, narrated from him (peace be upon him): "Usury (الرّبا) is of two types: one is lawful (حلال) and the other is unlawful (حرام). As for the lawful (الحلال), it is when a man lends his brother a loan (قرضاً) hoping that he will increase it and compensate him with more than what he took, without any condition between them. If he gives him more than what he took, without a condition between them, then it is permissible (مباح) for him, but he will have no reward with Allah for what he loaned. This is His saying: فَلا يَرْبُوا عِنْدَ اللهِ ** (it will not increase with Allah.) And as for the unlawful (الحرام), it is when a man gives a loan (قرضاً) and stipulates that he must return more than what he took. This is the unlawful (الحرام)."

And in al-Majma', narrated from Imam al-Baqir (peace be upon him): "It is when a man gives a gift (العطيّة) or presents a present (الهديّة) so that he may be recompensed with more than it. There is neither reward (أجر) nor burden (وزر) in it." وَما آتَيْتُمْ مِنْ زَكاةٍ تُرِيدُونَ وَجْهَ اللهِ ** (but whatever you pay as zakat, seeking Allah’s Face—) seeking His Face purely, فَأُولئِكَ هُمُ الْمُضْعِفُونَ ** (it is they who shall receive a manifold increase.) those who possess manifold increases (الاضعاف) of reward (الثواب) in the Hereafter (الأجل) and wealth (المال) in this world (العاجل).

Al-Qummi said: Meaning, whatever good you do for your brothers and whatever you loan them, not out of greed for increase.

And Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him) said: "Written on the gate of Paradise is: The loan (القرض) is rewarded eighteenfold, and charity (الصدقة) tenfold."

And in al-Majma', narrated from the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him): "Allah made the prayer (الصلاة) obligatory as a purification from arrogance (الكبر), and al-Zakat as a means (تسبيباً) for sustenance (الرزق)." And in al-Faqih, narrated from Fatimah (peace be upon her): That which is close to it (this meaning).

(٤٠) اللهُ الَّذِي خَلَقَكُمْ ثُمَّ رَزَقَكُمْ ثُمَّ يُمِيتُكُمْ ثُمَّ يُحْيِيكُمْ هَلْ مِنْ شُرَكائِكُمْ مَنْ يَفْعَلُ مِنْ ذلِكُمْ مِنْ شَيْءٍ سُبْحانَهُ وَتَعالى عَمَّا يُشْرِكُونَ ** (40) Allah is He who created you, then provided for you, then He makes you die, then He revives you. Is there anyone among your partners who can do any of these things? Immaculate is He, and exalted above what they associate [with Him]! And it was recited with the tā' (بالتّاء). In Misbah al-Shari'ah, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him), he said: The Messenger of Allah (may Allah bless him and his progeny) said: The greedy person (الحريص) is deprived (محروم). And despite his deprivation, he is blameworthy (مذموم) in whatever matter he is involved. And how can he not be deprived when he has fled from the covenant (وثاق) of Allah and opposed the saying of Allah, the Almighty, where He says: اللهُ الَّذِي خَلَقَكُمْ ثُمَّ رَزَقَكُمْ** (40) Allah is He who created you, then provided for you,

(٤١) ظَهَرَ الْفَسادُ فِي الْبَرِّ وَالْبَحْرِ بِما كَسَبَتْ أَيْدِي النَّاسِ. (Corruption has appeared in the land and the sea because of what the people’s hands have earned.)

Al-Qummi said: فِي الْبَرِّ (in the land) refers to the corruption (فساد) of the animals when it does not rain, and likewise the destruction (هلاك) of the sea creatures (دوابّ البحر) due to that. Al-Sadiq (peace be upon him) said: The life of the sea creatures (دواب البحر) is dependent on the rain. So when the rain ceases, ظَهَرَ الْفَسادُ فِي الْبَرِّ وَالْبَحْرِ (corruption has appeared in the land and the sea), and that is when sins (الذنوب) and acts of disobedience (المعاصي) become numerous.

And in al-Kafi and al-Qummi, narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him), he said: That, by Allah, was when the Ansar (الأنصار) said: "An Amir (أمير) from us and an Amir (أمير) from you." لِيُذِيقَهُمْ بَعْضَ الَّذِي عَمِلُوا (that He may make them taste a part of what they have done)—meaning, a part of its recompense (جزائه), for its completion (تمامه) is in the Hereafter— لَعَلَّهُمْ يَرْجِعُونَ (that they may return) from what they are upon.

(٤٢) قُلْ سِيرُوا فِي الْأَرْضِ فَانْظُرُوا كَيْفَ كانَ عاقِبَةُ الَّذِينَ مِنْ قَبْلُ ** (Say, 'Travel in the land and observe how was the fate of those who were before.') so that you may witness the truth (مصداق) of that. In al-Kafi, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): that He was asked about this Ayah, and He said: "What is meant by that is: Look into the Quran and know how was the fate of those who were before you, and what He informed you about." كانَ أَكْثَرُهُمْ مُشْرِكِينَ ** (Most of them were polytheists.) meaning, the evil of their fate was due to the prevalence (فشو) of polytheism (الشرك) among them.

(٤٣) فَأَقِمْ وَجْهَكَ لِلدِّينِ الْقَيِّمِ ** (43) So set your heart on the upright religion the one whose uprightness (الاستقامة) is profound مِنْ قَبْلِ أَنْ يَأْتِيَ يَوْمٌ لا مَرَدَّ لَهُ مِنَ اللهِ ** before there comes a day which cannot be averted from Allah. due to the certainty (لتحتّم) of its coming يَوْمَئِذٍ يَصَّدَّعُونَ On that day they will be separated. they will be separated (يتصدّعون), meaning they will be dispersed (يتفرّقون): a group in Paradise (الجنّة) and a group in the Blazing Fire (السّعير).

(٤٤) مَنْ كَفَرَ فَعَلَيْهِ كُفْرُهُ ** (44) Whoever disbelieves, his disbelief shall be against him. meaning, its evil consequence (وباله), which is the eternal Fire. وَمَنْ عَمِلَ صالِحاً فَلِأَنْفُسِهِمْ يَمْهَدُونَ ** and whoever acts righteously, they prepare the ground for themselves, meaning, they arrange their dwellings (منازلهم) in Paradise.

In al-Majma', narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him), he said: Indeed, the righteous deed (العمل الصالح) drives its doer to Paradise and prepares the ground for him, just as the servant (خادمه) of one of you prepares his bed (فراشه) for him.

(٤٥) لِيَجْزِيَ الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا وَعَمِلُوا الصَّالِحاتِ مِنْ فَضْلِهِ إِنَّهُ لا يُحِبُّ الْكافِرِينَ ** (45) That He may repay those who have faith and do righteous deeds out of His grace. Indeed, He does not love the faithless. He sufficed Himself from mentioning their reward (جزائهم) by implication (بالفحوى).

(٤٦) وَمِنْ آياتِهِ أَنْ يُرْسِلَ الرِّياحَ ** (46) And among His signs is that He sends the winds The winds of mercy (رياح الرَّحمة) مُبَشِّراتٍ ** with the rain (بالمطر) وَلِيُذِيقَكُمْ مِنْ رَحْمَتِهِ the benefits (المنافع) that follow them وَلِتَجْرِيَ الْفُلْكُ بِأَمْرِهِ وَلِتَبْتَغُوا مِنْ فَضْلِهِ ** meaning, sea trade (تجارة البحر) وَلَعَلَّكُمْ تَشْكُرُونَ ** and so that you may thank the favor (نعمة) of Allah therein.

وَلَقَدْ أَرْسَلْنا مِنْ قَبْلِكَ رُسُلاً إِلى قَوْمِهِمْ فَجاؤُهُمْ بِالْبَيِّناتِ فَانْتَقَمْنا مِنَ الَّذِينَ أَجْرَمُوا ** (47) Certainly We sent apostles before you to their people. They brought them manifest proofs. Then We took vengeance upon those who committed crimes. — by destruction (بالتَّدمير). وَكانَ حَقًّا عَلَيْنا نَصْرُ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ ** (47) And it was a duty incumbent upon Us to help the faithful. — In this is an indication that the vengeance was for their sake, and a manifestation of their honor, since He made them deserving upon Allah that He should grant them victory.

In al-Majma', narrated from the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم: "There is no Muslim man who defends the honor (عَرْض) of his brother except that it is a duty incumbent upon Allah that He should keep the Fire of Hell away from him on the Day of Resurrection." Then He recited: وَكانَ حَقًّا عَلَيْنا نَصْرُ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ. (47) And it was a duty incumbent upon Us to help the faithful.

And in al-Faqih, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq عليه السلام, he said: "It is sufficient help (نُصْرَة) for the believer that he sees his enemy committing acts of disobedience to Allah."

(٤٨) **اللهُ الَّذِي يُرْسِلُ الرِّياحَ فَتُثِيرُ سَحاباً ** (48) It is Allah who sends the winds, so they stir up clouds, Al-Qummi said: meaning, He raises it.

**فَيَبْسُطُهُ فِي السَّماءِ كَيْفَ يَشاءُ ** then He spreads it in the sky however He wishes, moving and stationary, covering completely and not covering completely, from one side without the other, and so forth.

وَيَجْعَلُهُ كِسَفاً ** and He turns it into fragments. It is said: fragments (قطعاً). Meaning, He spreads it sometimes, and other times He turns it (يَجْعَلُهُ) into fragments (قطعاً). And Al-Qummi said: some of it upon some (i.e., piled up).

فَتَرَى الْوَدْقَ ** Then you see the rain يَخْرُجُ مِنْ خِلالِهِ issuing from its midst. And in al-Majma', narrated from Ali (peace be upon him): from its gaps (من خلله).

فَإِذا أَصابَ بِهِ مَنْ يَشاءُ مِنْ عِبادِهِ إِذا هُمْ يَسْتَبْشِرُونَ Then when He causes it to fall on whomever He wishes of His servants, behold, they rejoice. meaning, their lands and their territories. Behold, they rejoice (إِذا هُمْ يَسْتَبْشِرُونَ) at the coming of abundance (الخصب).

(٤٩) وَإِنْ كَانُوا مِنْ قَبْلِ أَنْ يُنَزَّلَ عَلَيْهِمْ ** (meaning) the rain (المطر) مِنْ قَبْلِهِ ** (is) a repetition for emphasis (لِلتَّأْكِيد) لَمُبْلِسِينَ ** (meaning) despairing (لابسين). وَإِنْ كَانُوا مِنْ قَبْلِ أَنْ يُنَزَّلَ عَلَيْهِمْ مِنْ قَبْلِهِ لَمُبْلِسِينَ (49) though they were before it was sent down upon them, before that, in despair. (٥٠) فَانْظُرْ إِلى آثارِ رَحْمَتِ اللهِ ** (meaning) the effect of the rain (الغيث) in the form of vegetation (النبات), trees (الأشجار), and various kinds of fruits (الثمار). And it was recited (وقرئ) as آثار (آثار). كَيْفَ يُحْيِ الْأَرْضَ بَعْدَ مَوْتِها إِنَّ ذلِكَ ** (meaning) He Who has power (قَدَرَ) to revive the earth after its death لَمُحْيِ الْمَوْتى ** (meaning) He will surely revive them (لَيُحْيِيَهُمْ) without fail (لَا مَحَالَة) وَهُوَ عَلى كُلِّ شَيْءٍ قَدِيرٌ فَانْظُرْ إِلَى آثَارِ رَحْمَتِ اللَّهِ كَيْفَ يُحْيِي الْأَرْضَ بَعْدَ مَوْتِهَا إِنَّ ذَلِكَ لَمُحْيِي الْمَوْتَى وَهُوَ عَلَى كُلِّ شَيْءٍ قَدِيرٌ (50) So observe the effects of Allah’s mercy: how He revives the earth after its death. Indeed, He is the reviver of the dead, and He has power over all things.

(51) وَلَئِنْ أَرْسَلْنا رِيحاً فَرَأَوْهُ مُصْفَرًّا ** (51) And if We send a wind, and they see it yellowed, It was said they saw the effect and the crop, for it is indicated by what preceded. And it was said (it refers to) the cloud (السحاب), because if it was yellowed, it would not rain. لَظَلُّوا مِنْ بَعْدِهِ يَكْفُرُونَ ** they will surely continue to disbelieve after that. It was said these verses are lamenting the disbelievers (الكفّار) due to their lack of steadfastness, their lack of reflection, their swift wavering due to their lack of contemplation, and their poor judgment. For the sound view necessitates that they rely upon Allah and seek refuge in Him through seeking forgiveness (بالاستغفار) if the rain (القطر) is withheld from them, and that they do not despair of His mercy. And that they hasten to gratitude (الشكر) and persistence (الاستدامة) through obedience (بالطاعة) if He afflicts them with His mercy, and that they do not exceed the bounds in rejoicing (الاستبشار). And that they be patient regarding His trial if He strikes their crops with yellowing (بالاصفرار), and and that they do not deny His favors.

(٥٢) فَإِنَّكَ لا تُسْمِعُ الْمَوْتى ** (52) Indeed, you cannot make the dead hear, And they are like them when they blocked their senses (مشاعرهم) from the truth. وَلا تُسْمِعُ الصُّمَّ الدُّعاءَ (nor can you make the deaf hear the call) And it was read with the yāʾ (ي) having a fatḥah and al-ṣumm (الصمّ) in the nominative case (rafʿ). إِذا وَلَّوْا مُدْبِرِينَ (when they turn their backs and flee.) It was said that He restricted the ruling to make the impossibility more severe, because the deaf person who is facing you (المقبل), even if he does not hear the speech, understands something from it through movements.

(٥٣) وَما أَنْتَ بِهادِي الْعُمْيِ عَنْ ضَلالَتِهِمْ إِنْ تُسْمِعُ إِلَّا مَنْ يُؤْمِنُ بِآياتِنا ** (53) You cannot guide the blind out of their error. You can make none hear except those who believe in Our signs. Because he is the one who receives the utterance and ponders the meaning, فَهُمْ مُسْلِمُونَ ** (so they are submitters) to what you command them with. (٥٤) اللهُ الَّذِي خَلَقَكُمْ مِنْ ضَعْفٍ ** (54) It is Allah who created you from a state of weakness, He originated you weak, or He created you from a weak origin, which is the sperm-drop (النّطفة), ثُمَّ جَعَلَ مِنْ بَعْدِ ضَعْفٍ قُوَّةً (then He appointed, after weakness, strength), which is your reaching maturity (الاشدّ) ثُمَّ جَعَلَ مِنْ بَعْدِ قُوَّةٍ ضَعْفاً وَشَيْبَةً ** (then after strength He brought about weakness and old age) when age takes hold of you. يَخْلُقُ ما يَشاءُ ** (He creates whatever He wishes) of weakness, strength, and old age. And it was recited with the fathah on the ḍād in all of them [i.e., ضَعَف instead of ضَعْف]. وَهُوَ الْعَلِيمُ الْقَدِيرُ.** (and He is the All-knowing, the All-powerful.)

(٥٥) وَيَوْمَ تَقُومُ السَّاعَةُ ** (And on the day when the Hour rises) The Resurrection (القيامة), and it is one of the predominant names (for that Day). يُقْسِمُ الْمُجْرِمُونَ (the guilty will swear) that they did not remain (in the world or in the graves) غَيْرَ ساعَةٍ ** (but an hour) They deemed the duration of their stay short. كَذلِكَ (Thus): meaning, like that turning away (التصرّف), narrated from al-Saduq (الصدوق). كانُوا يُؤْفَكُونَ (they used to be turned away): meaning, they were turned away (يصرفون) in the world.

(٥٦) وَقالَ الَّذِينَ أُوتُوا الْعِلْمَ وَالْإِيمانَ. (56) And those who were given knowledge and faith will say: In al-Kafi and al-Uyun, narrated from al-Rida (peace be upon him) in the tradition where he describes the Imamate and the Imam, he said: "So He (Allah) entrusted it (the Imamate) to Ali صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم بأمر الله عزّ وجلّ (by the command of Allah, the Mighty and Majestic) according to the pattern (رسم) that Allah the Almighty prescribed. Thus, it became established in his pure descendants (ذرّيته الأصفياء) whom Allah the Almighty granted knowledge (العلم) and faith (الإيمان), according to His saying: وَقالَ الَّذِينَ أُوتُوا الْعِلْمَ وَالْإِيمانَ (And those who were given knowledge and faith will say) [the rest of] the Ayah." لَقَدْ لَبِثْتُمْ فِي كِتابِ اللهِ ** (You have certainly stayed in Allah’s Book) [meaning] in His knowledge (علمه), His decree (قضائه), and what He made incumbent upon you and wrote down, إِلى يَوْمِ الْبَعْثِ ** (until the Day of Resurrection) which you denied. فَهذا يَوْمُ الْبَعْثِ وَلكِنَّكُمْ كُنْتُمْ لا تَعْلَمُونَ ** (This is the Day of Resurrection, but you did not know.) that it is true, due to your negligence (تفريطكم) in reflection (النظر). Thus, the invalidity (بطلان) of your denial has become clear to you. Al-Qummi [said]: This Ayah has a transposition (مقدّمة ومؤخّرة), and it is actually: وَقالَ الَّذِينَ أُوتُوا الْعِلْمَ وَالْإِيمانَ لَقَدْ لَبِثْتُمْ فِي كِتابِ اللهِ إِلى يَوْمِ الْبَعْثِ.**

(٥٧) فَيَوْمَئِذٍ لا يَنْفَعُ الَّذِينَ ظَلَمُوا مَعْذِرَتُهُمْ ** (57) Then on that day the excuse of those who did wrong will not benefit them, وقرئ بالياء وَلا هُمْ يُسْتَعْتَبُونَ ** And it was read with the yā' (ي). nor will they be asked to make amends. They will not be called to that which necessitates their seeking satisfaction (إعتابهم), meaning, the removal of their blame (عتبهم) through repentance (التوبة) and obedience (الطاعة), just as they were called to it in the world (الدنيا). [This is derived] from their saying: "So-and-so sought my satisfaction (استعتبني), so I satisfied him (فأعتبته)," meaning, "he sought my pleasure (استرضاني), so I pleased him (فأرضيته)."

(٥٨) وَلَقَدْ ضَرَبْنا لِلنَّاسِ فِي هذَا الْقُرْآنِ مِنْ كُلِّ مَثَلٍ وَلَئِنْ جِئْتَهُمْ بِآيَةٍ لَيَقُولَنَّ الَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا ** (58) And We have certainly drawn for mankind in this Quran every kind of parable. And if you bring them a sign, those who are faithless will surely say: Due to the extremity of their obstinacy (عنادهم) and the hardness of their hearts, إِنْ أَنْتُمْ ** They mean the Messenger and the believers, إِلَّا مُبْطِلُونَ ** Falsifiers (مزوّرون).

(٥٩) كَذلِكَ يَطْبَعُ اللهُ عَلَى قُلُوبِ الَّذِينَ لا يَعْلَمُونَ. (59) Thus does Allah seal the hearts of those who do not know. (٦٠) فَاصْبِرْ على أذاهم إِنَّ وَعْدَ اللهِ بنصرتك وإظهار دينك على الدّين كلّه حَقُّ لا بدّ من إنجازه وَلا يَسْتَخِفَّنَّكَ الَّذِينَ لا يُوقِنُونَ ** (60) So be patient; Allah’s promise is indeed true. And do not let those who are not certain make you unsteady. So be patient regarding their harm (أذاهم). Allah’s promise is indeed true—[the promise] of granting you victory and making your religion prevail over all other religions—it is a truth (حَقٌّ) that must inevitably be fulfilled (إنجازه). And do not let those who are not certain make you unsteady—and do not let them incite you to levity (الخفّة) and anxiety (القلق) through their denial (تكذيبهم) and their harming (إيذائهم), for they are doubters (شاكّون), misguided (ضالّون), and such behavior is not considered strange (لا يستبدع) from them. And al-Qummi [said]: meaning, "do not let them anger you (لا يغضبك)." And the reward (ثواب) for reciting this Surah has been mentioned previously. O Allah, grant us the provision (ارزقنا) of reciting it, by the right of Muhammad and his Household (peace be upon them).

سُورَة لُقْمانَ مكيّة

Narrated from Ibn ‘Abbās: Three verses were revealed in Medina: وَلَوْ أَنَّ ما فِي الْأَرْضِ (And if all the trees on the earth...) until the end of them. And the number of its verses (آيها) is thirty-three verses according to the Hijazi count (حجازيّ), and thirty-four in the remaining counts (الباقين). بِسْمِ اللهِ الرَّحْمنِ الرَّحِيمِ. (In the Name of Allah, the All-beneficent, the All-merciful.)
